headline: Palm Oil Prices Crash 28% Below 2024 Peak
context: Crude palm oil futures fell to RM3,200 per tonne in March 2026 from RM4,450 in mid-2024, squeezing FELDA settlers and plantation workers across Sabah and Sarawak.

cards.big: At RM3,200 per tonne, 112,000 FELDA settlers earn below minimum viability threshold
cards.sub: Smallholders need RM3,800 just to cover fertiliser, labour, and loan repayments

cards.big: Palm oil contributes 5.2% to GDP and RM85 billion in export earnings annually
cards.sub: Every RM500 per tonne drop wipes RM9 billion from national export revenue

cards.big: FELDA settler average age is 62 — too old to pivot and too indebted to retire
cards.sub: Second-generation youth migration leaves 45% of FELDA schemes labour-deficient

cards.big: MPOB's price stabilisation fund holds only RM1.2 billion — enough for 4 months of support
cards.sub: Government has no long-term buffer mechanism comparable to Thailand's rubber fund

cards.big: Palm oil dependency is Malaysia's version of the resource curse — booms mask the failure to diversify
Should FELDA settlers receive guaranteed minimum income of RM2,500 per month regardless of CPO prices?
